description: Rubric category to external/internal source traceability map for ORB Residential Quality Lab.
disclaimer: Internal quality indicator, not a regulatory judgement. Aligned to recognised statutory, regulatory and professional sources where applicable.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output foregrounds child voice, presentation, communication and welfare-focused subject language.
categories.rationale: Children's homes quality standards and NICE looked-after children guidance emphasise wishes, feelings and child-centred practice. IndiCare principles operationalise this in recording markers — child voice, presentation, wishes/feelings and the child's experience before, during and after adult support.
categories.scoring_caution: Marker-based detection may miss nuanced child voice; high scores do not prove child participation in real practice. ORB must not invent feelings — scaffolds prompt for missing voice rather than fabricating it.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output avoids inventing facts not in input and marks unknown information appropriately.
categories.rationale: Accurate, defensible records are expected in inspection and professional standards. ORB enforces no-invention via pattern checks and missing-information markers.
categories.scoring_caution: Cannot detect subtle invention or omission; fixture mode may not reflect live LLM behaviour.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether wording avoids blaming, punitive or diagnostic language and uses respectful residential phrasing.
categories.rationale: Trauma-informed and relational practice guidance supports non-punitive recording. Pattern lists are internally curated and require professional review for completeness.
categories.scoring_caution: Regex patterns cannot capture all harmful phrasing. Wording rewrite scenarios must reframe poor input rather than pass blaming language through to outputs.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output separates observable facts from unsupported interpretation or assumption.
categories.rationale: Professional recording standards require factual clarity. ORB uses keyword heuristics that may miss subtle interpretive overreach.
categories.scoring_caution: Heuristic scoring; independent reviewers should test edge cases with practitioners. Rewards reflection markers (may indicate, may have communicated) and observation separation when genuinely present — not rubric inflation.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether adult actions, support offered, de-escalation and attuned response are visible in the record.
categories.rationale: Positive relationships and adult support are core quality standard themes. Practice-informed models inform relational recording without clinical claims.
categories.scoring_caution: Marker presence does not assess quality of support delivered, only whether it is recorded. Scaffolds prompt for missing adult action rather than inventing it.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether outcome, repair, next steps and follow-up actions are documented.
categories.rationale: Inspection and safeguarding guidance expect chronology, outcomes and follow-through to be visible in records.
categories.scoring_caution: Follow-up markers may be present without meaningful action planning.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether safeguarding escalation, DSL/manager notification and appropriate boundaries appear when scenario requires them.
categories.rationale: Statutory safeguarding guidance and protection quality standard underpin escalation expectations. ORB flags failure to escalate and inappropriate investigation guidance.
categories.scoring_caution: Cannot assess whether real-world escalation was correct; only whether record reflects pathway language.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether manager oversight, debrief, Reg 44 or supervision themes appear when required by scenario.
categories.rationale: Leadership and management quality standard and Reg 44/45 inspection context require visible oversight and learning.
categories.scoring_caution: Does not verify management actions occurred; only recording visibility.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output is structured, proportionate length, and actionable for shift handover use.
categories.rationale: Inspection evidence expectations imply usable chronology and records. Length/structure heuristics are internally defined and need practitioner validation.
categories.scoring_caution: Too short/too long thresholds are governance choices, not statutory requirements.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether residential record tone is professional, balanced and free of blaming or clinical overreach.
categories.rationale: Professional standards and quality standards expect respectful, defensible recording tone.
categories.scoring_caution: Partially derived from blaming/diagnostic flag absence; may over-score neutral but unhelpful text.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output avoids unnecessary identifiable information patterns (IDs, phone numbers, postcodes, NHS numbers).
categories.rationale: Data minimisation is a statutory principle. ORB pattern checks operationalise internal privacy rules.
categories.scoring_caution: Pattern-based; cannot guarantee full anonymisation or lawful basis compliance.

categories.what_orb_scores: Whether output includes adult review caveats and avoids replacing professional judgement or guaranteeing compliance.
categories.rationale: Professional accountability and safeguarding role boundaries require adults to decide and sign off. Core ORB ethical boundary.
categories.scoring_caution: Presence of 'draft only' text does not ensure practitioner review occurred.
